title: Syncalathium disciforme
taxonomy: Syncalathium disciforme is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Syncalathium, which is a group of plants native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is further classified as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae, which includes thistle-like plants. Syncalathium disciforme is a perennial herb that grows in rocky and mountainous areas. Its classification is part of the plant's unique identity and helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and adaptations.

It is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
